title: Level 804
wikitext:
{{Decay Theme}}[[File:Outdated as frick.png|center|thumb|1x1px]] {{Blurbackground|file=Level 804.jpeg|opacity=0.75}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 804: "Noctourism." }}{{Headerbox|file=Level 804.jpeg|speed=50|header=“For yet the world crumples. But just because it dies, just because it crushes underneath us… doesn’t mean its prototype dies with it.“}} '''Level 804 is the 805th level of the Backrooms.'''
{{Class 3}} == Description. == Level 804, also known as "Noctourism," is an extremely dark road that winds through a forest. Strange, warped, and unsettling signs are scattered everywhere, featuring things that seem impossible but exist nonetheless. The sky is pitch black, lacking stars, a moon, or any light source, giving the illusion of danger that isn’t there. Crossing the whole level is a road, this road continuously splits into hundreds of different paths, and each path branches off into hundreds more, making it nearly impossible to know where you are headed, In the center of the road, it diverge in three-way splits, creating a Y-shaped pattern. This results in countless paths to explore, each with unique signs, trees, and surroundings. Overall, the road network looks like the branches and roots of a tree. No one knows for sure why it formed this way, but some think it might have been intended to maximize space in the forest. {{Blurimage|width=500px|file=Level 804.jpeg|caption=The forest, with a road splitting into 2 paths, and a broken and corrupted sign.}} === Landmarks === The Landmarks of this level are locations of different architectural features than everything else, like a Building in a neighborhood. These landmarks are popular habitats for people or outpost groups who want to find a secure, nice place. They are also hideouts for entities, making it a numbers game between danger and life. They usually one hide at the back of the structures, though, making some places perfectly safe, and some a 50/50. This is why hardly anyone goes anywhere but the Gas station, since that's the largest and safest, but some people don't even go there at all.{{Image Banner|image=Level 804 bus.jpg}} The first landmark is the Bus Stop, which looks like a normal bus stop, except for its location; it sits at the edge of a sidewalk, next to a single streetlamp. The bus stop is almost entirely covered with layers of newspapers containing information about various Backrooms levels, and researchers think these newspapers are where some of that information was first found. But don't get your hopes up, finding the bus stop is very difficult because it sometimes moves without warning. This is also the reason why only a few photographs of it exist: it switches locations every 12 hours, and observations indicate that the newspapers covering the stop’s walls change every 12 hours too, even when not at the bus stop itself. {{Blurimage|file=Level 804 bus.jpg|width=500px|caption=The bus stop, sitting on a stone pathway, with the stop light nearby.}}{{Image Banner|image=Level 804 gas station.jpeg}}The next landmark is the Gas Station. It is located near the level's spawn point and is one of the most visited spots in Level 804. Next to the station is a shopping area called Gamma Mall. As wanderers enter, they see flickering yellow lights and an interior that seems much larger than the outside, possibly even infinite. The aisles contain strange items from across the Backrooms, including bottles of [[Object 1|Almond Water]] to [[Entity 700|entire vehicles]]. Despite the odd inventory, most items work normally. The cashier is a Faceling, currently the only known being in this level. It looks constantly tired, speaks with a strong Irish accent, and talks at about half the speed of an average person. Surprisingly, it does its job well. If a wanderer tries to buy something, the Faceling will scan it, and the item can then be taken and used as intended, as long as the station's systems don’t fail. The main draw of the station is its fuel pumps. Instead of gasoline, they hold Almond Water, which often overflows. Wanderers frequently drink from the pumps, believing it helps keep them healthy and ready for the levels ahead.{{Blurimage|file=Level 804 gas station.jpeg|caption=The gas station outside the Gamma mall, with the posters car to the far left.|width=500px}}{{Image Banner|image=Level 804 pathways.jpeg}}The final landmark is the Pathways. These massive trails connect roads that can be over 50 miles apart. At first glance, they look like desire paths, but there is no proof that anyone has ever walked on them. Even stranger, they are lined with working streetlamps, lighting up routes that seemingly nobody uses. Researchers are still looking into the origin of these pathways, but one thing is clear: ordinary human activity could not have created them. Next to them, most of the trails are surrounded by dense forests of trees that reach nearly 20 feet high, which adds more to the unsettling atmosphere, making wanderers stress even more. Several wanderers have reported hearing faint sounds from about 50 meters away. People often describe the noises as distant giggling, barely noticeable through the forest. Others claim to have seen pairs of eyes watching them from between the trees. These reports do not match current records since no entities have been officially documented in the area. However, the consistency of the sightings has raised concerns among researchers. Whether the sounds and sightings come from an undiscovered entity or something else entirely is still a mystery.{{Blurimage|width=500px|file=Level 804 pathways.jpeg|caption=The pathways, specifically the closest one to the spawning point.}} == Entities == There is no entities in the level except 2, 1 discovered, 1 claimed === The cashier === The cashier is a Teenager faceling, he is the only person inside of the Gamma mall, where “people” buy all the time. When in an interview, he said this: “So, I was just doing my job, until this guy came and just, hit a can of Pepsi on my head! I was like, HEY! And he went, “Ohhhh, what are you gonna do about it”… So I jumped over the counter.” This is a sign of 2 things, that the cashier has anger issues, and that he has been here for a while for that reaction, when asked, he said he started working at the store in 2012, which when you count him being 17 now, he was just 3 when he started working. Other info is that he secretly steals items from the shop while customers aren’t here, he even claims he has 12 cars! All other info has been lost from the call due to the corruption of the audio in [[Level 12]]. === The Giggling. === The giggling are claimed entity’s to be in the forests, they come in groups of 4 and watch a person from the distance, giggling in the distance. A couple of people have gone missing while walking in the pathways, and it was most likely them. They are claimed to be over 9 feet tall, with body parts where they aren’t meant to be, and organs poking out of their hands. They have no eyes, just teeth, they use echolocation to move, which humans only hear as chattering. They aren’t blind though, it’s just more effective and efficient than seeing. They are also claimed to be able to float up to 2 feet off the ground, which only the elders posses to move. The cashier also claims to hear giggling coming from the back door. Noting that he was CLEARLY being watched. == Colony’s / outposts == There is only a single Colony, hiding in the safest place from the Giggling. === David’s gang === David’s gang is a gang or mafia, specifically designed to quote on quote, “Bring those stupid gigglers to justice.” Since that statement was created, they have currently killed over 43 Gigglers, using lots of weapons such as machine Guns and Grenades. They reside inside the corners of the Gamma mall, grabbing food, water, and anything that can hurt the gigglers, and force the cashier to let them have it for free, beside themselves, the cashier is their only friend. They also steal cars and drive around drunk sometimes, currently having run over 2 gigglers while driving. in total. == Entrances and exits == === Entrances === * Traveling until the fog goes dark in [[Level 105]] will lead you to the gas station (back door), travel to the front door and you will be sent to this level. * Ditto, but with [[Level 164]]. * Noclipping through a computer in [[Level 4]] will send you to this level. * Nocliping through a garden gnome in [[User:Vuncle/Level 803|Level 803]] will send you here. === Exits === * Buying any sort of game will send you to [[Level 25]] * Going far beyond the parameters of the level and surviving the gigglers will send you to [[Level 131]] * Touching a pink tree will send you to [[Level 757]]. * Digging into the ground next to the gas station will send you to [[Level 154|STATION #154]] * {{Redirect|Prev=803|Next=805}} [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Darkness]] [[Category:Forest]]
